title: Astragalus alpinus subsp. brunetianus
what_description: Astragalus alpinus subsp. brunetianus is a subspecies of the alpine milk-vetch plant, belonging to the family Fabaceae. It is a perennial herb with a woody base, typically 5-15 cm tall, with a few stems and leaves. The leaves are alternate, ovate to lanceolate, 2-4 cm long, and glabrous, while the flowers are pinkish-purple, 6-8 mm long, and in a raceme. The fruit is an indehiscent legume, 6-10 mm long, containing 1-2 seeds. This subspecies is endemic to the mountainous regions of the Iberian Peninsula.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Astragalus alpinus subsp. brunetianus are inflammation, which it reduces through its anti-inflammatory properties, and fatigue, as it boosts energy by stimulating the production of red blood cells. Additionally, Astragalus alpinus subsp. brunetianus has been shown to help manage diabetes by improving insulin sensitivity and lowering blood sugar levels, and hypertension through its ability to relax blood vessels and reduce blood pressure. It also helps alleviate anemia by increasing the production of red blood cells and has been found to be effective in treating asthma by reducing inflammation in the airways. Furthermore, Astragalus alpinus subsp. brunetianus has been demonstrated to have anti-cancer properties and is effective in preventing digestive issues by reducing inflammation and improving gut health. Its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties also make it effective in treating various other ailments, including arthritis, cardiovascular disease, bronchitis, immune deficiency, gastrointestinal issues, gastritis, kidney disease, gastroenteritis, respiratory issues, autoimmune disorders, and skin issues.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Astragalus alpinus subsp. brunetianus are its antioxidant activity, which is attributed to flavonoids and phenolic acids, that help protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. It also exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which are responsible for its ability to modulate the immune response and reduce inflammation, due to the presence of triterpenoid saponins and alkaloids. Additionally, the plant has immunomodulatory effects, stimulating the immune system and enhancing its ability to fight infections, thanks to the bioactive compounds such as alkaloids and phenolic acids. Furthermore, Astragalus alpinus subsp. brunetianus displays antimicrobial properties, inhibiting the growth of various pathogens, including bacteria and fungi, due to the presence of volatile oils and phenolic acids. Its cardioprotective effects are also significant, helping to protect against cardiovascular disease by reducing lipid peroxidation and improving endothelial function, attributed to flavonoids and phenolic acids.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Astragalus alpinus subsp. brunetianus are astragaloside IV, astragaloside II, astragaloside VI, astragaloside III, and astragaloside VIII. These saponins possess significant immunomodulatory, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. Astragaloside IV has been shown to inhibit T cell activation and proliferation, while astragaloside II and VI exhibit anti-inflammatory effects by suppressing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines. Astragaloside III has been found to have antioxidant properties, scavenging free radicals and reducing oxidative stress. Astragaloside VIII has been reported to have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, suggesting its potential in treating autoimmune diseases and inflammation-related conditions.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Astragalus alpinus subsp. brunetianus are astragalus tincture, a concentrated liquid solution of the herb's roots and stems in a solvent such as ethanol or glycerin, astragalus infusion, a tea made by steeping the dried roots and stems in hot water, decoction, a liquid extract obtained by boiling the roots and stems in water, and dried herbs tea, a loose-leaf tea made from the dried roots and stems. Astragalus capsules are also prepared by filling gelatin or vegetable capsules with the dried roots and stems. These preparations are made to take advantage of the herb's adaptogenic properties, including its ability to stimulate the immune system and reduce inflammation. The herb's roots and stems are rich in saponins, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds that are believed to contribute to its medicinal effects. The various preparations of Astragalus alpinus subsp. brunetianus can be used to support immune function, reduce stress, and promote overall well-being.
side_effects_description: The possible side effect of using Astragalus alpinus subsp. brunetianus are numerous and can be severe when used improperly. It may interact with medications such as blood thinners, diabetes medications, and blood pressure medications, which can lead to adverse effects like increased bleeding, low blood sugar, and high blood pressure. Additionally, Astragalus alpinus subsp. brunetianus can cause gastrointestinal issues like vomiting and diarrhea due to its emetic and purgative properties. Allergic reactions such as skin rashes, itching, and difficulty breathing can also occur in some individuals. Furthermore, Astragalus alpinus subsp. brunetianus may cause liver damage due to its potential toxicity, particularly when consumed in large quantities or for extended periods.
